product_context: A homepage for a collaborative planning workspace used by product, design, and operations teams to align docs, tasks, roadmaps, and recurring reviews.
design_goals: Make a broad workspace product feel coherent and easy to adopt.
Show several workflows without losing focus or becoming card soup.
Feel polished and modular rather than expressive for its own sake.
constraints: Responsive across desktop and mobile with mobile-specific prioritization.
Avoid direct visual overlap with Notion or Airbnb.
Use reusable modules instead of many bespoke sections.
Keep implementation practical in a standard web app stack.
layout_rules: Use a stable shell with a few repeatable module types rather than many bespoke sections.
Alternate between promise, proof, and interaction modules to maintain pace.
Let mobile reprioritize content and interaction patterns instead of only stacking desktop layouts.
typography_rules: Use concise headlines and tightly controlled support copy.
Rely on hierarchy, weight, and spacing rather than expressive display typography everywhere.
Keep supporting labels quiet but legible.
component_rules: Use a modular hero, one calculator or quantified-proof block, one selector-driven workflow section, and one structured footer.
Design around repeatable cards and product-like modules.
Keep one dominant CTA visible throughout the hero and first proof section.
motion_rules: Use motion to switch panes, expand views, or confirm module changes.
Keep autoplay and carousel behavior explicit and controllable.
Reduce motion intensity when clarity matters more than spectacle.
copy_rules: Translate platform value into team tasks, alignment, and time saved.
Use simple action labels such as Try free, Request a demo, Explore workflows, or See pricing.
accessibility_rules: Use semantic tabs, buttons, inputs, and lists for modules.
Preserve focus order across cards, overlays, and selectors.
Make any calculator or carousel controls keyboard-safe and screen-reader clear.
performance_rules: Code-split heavier media or interactive modules.
Prefer a small number of reusable shells over many custom interactions.
Defer noncritical media until user intent is clear.
anti_patterns: Overbuilding the page with too many equally weighted cards.
Using carousels where a simple stacked explanation would be clearer.
Making mobile a compressed desktop replica.
Using cost-savings math that feels inflated or untrustworthy.
notes: Recommended stack: Next.js or Vite with React, plus a disciplined utility CSS or component system.
Recommended first build output: hero, modular workflow grid, selector-led feature pane, quantified proof section, testimonial or trust module, footer.
